summary: Install lifecycle and provenance landed across Slices A (taxonomy + --self-check), B (manifest writer + --reconcile + fcntl.flock + migrations + INSTALL_LIFECYCLE.md), and C (per-file atomic copy, --upgrade, --uninstall, --check --include-templates, pre-install secrets scan, symlink refusal, --interactive, ADR). All 11 F10 acceptance criteria from the planning gate have falsifiable acceptance tests; 27 M9 tests green. Gate sign-offs: strategy-planner (decision-memo.md v2.1), architecture-red-team (review v1 + v2; v2 GREEN-LIGHT WITH CONDITIONS, all conditions shipped), code-reviewer (review-m9.md GREEN-LIGHT WITH CONDITIONS; 3 BLOCKERs and 4 MAJORs addressed in commits b9cbeb7 and 7055218).
suggested_commit_message: meta-M9: approve install lifecycle and provenance
reviewer_notes: Strategy-planner: decision-memo.md v2.1 — 5-class ownership taxonomy, integer schema_version with linear-chain migrations, plan-then-confirm upgrade, allowlist semantics, atomic-copy with orphan sweep, fcntl.flock per target
Architecture-red-team v1: 4 BLOCKERs (F1/F2/F3/F7) + 7 MAJORs surfaced; reject-and-replan with narrowly-scoped fix
Architecture-red-team v2: GREEN-LIGHT WITH CONDITIONS (NI-1, NI-2, Q2, NI-3); all four conditions shipped in v2.1 closeout commit
Code-reviewer (review-m9.md): GREEN-LIGHT WITH CONDITIONS — 3 BLOCKERs (scaffold-orphan undeclared; cmd_enrich DRY duplication; templates not actually used) and 4 MAJORs
Code-reviewer BLOCKERs all shipped in commits b9cbeb7 and 7055218
Code-reviewer MAJORs F4 and F5 addressed during the F2 consolidation
Code-reviewer MAJOR F6 (positive-path tests for --adopt/--rename-local/--accept-removal) shipped
Code-reviewer MAJOR F7 (partial-failure manifest staleness) documented in INSTALL_LIFECYCLE.md; engine recovers correctly on rerun

deferred_items.description: Move scaffold-canonical docs under a namespaced directory for additional human-readable separation.
Versioned scaffold releases and pinned-version upgrade target.
overlays: [] reserved per file entry; *.project.md reserved as ignored convention. M9.4 fills in concat semantics so customizations survive scaffold upgrades.
A project enriched with profile A wants to add profile B's agents without re-enriching everything.
These two files must stay byte-identical until a future Slice removes the scaffold-side duplicate. Currently a documented invariant; not yet mechanically enforced.
Engine could write a partial manifest reflecting succeeded copies before returning on a mid-run failure. Currently documented; recovery via re-run.
